links.key: bird-日本松雀鷹-113a6e5f__distributed_in__loc-温带阔叶混交林-fc0864f2
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Japanese sparrowhawk typically inhabits many forest types such as deciduous, coniferous and mixed, at elevations up to 1800 meters but usually below 1000 meters. It prefers southern taiga and subalpine zones near rivers. During migration and wintering, they can be seen in villages, and open areas where woodlands and shrubs mix with marshes and fields. The species can generally be found at its breeding grounds in eastern Russia, eastwards to Sakhalin, the Korean peninsula, Japan, and Northeastern China. Although, the distribution of the Japanese sparrowhawk varies by and within subspecies

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: gularis is the most widespread subspecies and is made up of two major populations. The first is migratory and breeds in northeast China, in the most eastern parts of Russia, as well as Japan. It winters in the Philippines and Indonesia

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The Japanese sparrowhawk typically inhabits many forest types such as deciduous, coniferous and mixed, at elevations up to 1800 meters but usually below 1000 meters. It prefers southern taiga and subalpine zones near rivers. During migration and wintering, they can be seen in villages, and open areas where woodlands and shrubs mix with marshes and fields. The species can generally be found at its breeding grounds in eastern Russia, eastwards to Sakhalin, the Korean peninsula, Japan, and Northeastern China. Although, the distribution of the Japanese sparrowhawk varies by and within subspecies
